name: install-cli-tools
purpose: Installs CLI tools (fzf, bat, fd, delta, yazi, etc.) on Windows via winget or GitHub release download; reads tool list from .claude/tool-manifest.json.
sanitize_fields: tool list sourced from .claude/tool-manifest.json (SoNash-specific tool set)
notes: module_system: none (bash). Windows-only gate (uname -s MINGW/MSYS/CYGWIN check). Hardcoded winget IDs and GitHub repo slugs are SoNash toolchain choices — these would be replaced for JASON-OS. Zip-slip protection implemented (extracts to subdirectory, checks for path traversal entries). Platform-portable architecture; tool list is the only sanitize-required element.

name: lighthouse-audit
purpose: Runs Lighthouse performance/accessibility/SEO/PWA audits against all SoNash application routes, saves JSON+HTML reports to .lighthouse/.
notes: module_system: cjs. ROUTES array is SoNash-specific (/, /today, /journal, /growth, /more, /login). Requires running app server on localhost:3000 or LIGHTHOUSE_BASE_URL. Product-code tooling — the pattern is portable (multi-page Lighthouse runner) but routes/URLs are SoNash-specific. Rated not-portable-product as it wraps SoNash frontend routes.

name: log-override
purpose: Logs overrides of blocking checks (SKIP_TRIGGERS, SKIP_PATTERNS, etc.) to .claude/override-log.jsonl as an accountability audit trail; supports --list, --clear, --analytics, --quick modes; auto-generates DEBT entries when bypass threshold exceeded.
notes: module_system: cjs. PIECE 1A GAP — this file was referenced in Piece 1a as missing from JASON-OS and has now been found. JSONL target: .claude/override-log.jsonl. Dual rotation: size-based (50KB) + entry-based (64KB threshold, keeps 60 of last 100 via rotate-state.js). Exports logSkip/logOverride/computeAnalytics/readEntries for programmatic use. C3-G3 auto-DEBT integration: 15+ bypasses in 14 days → writes to docs/technical-debt/MASTER_DEBT.jsonl (SoNash-specific path but schema is generic). Override entry schema: {timestamp, check, reason, user, cwd, git_branch} — user and cwd are redacted at write time. Depends on .claude/hooks/lib/sanitize-input for arg sanitization. All deps must co-port. This is a HIGH-PRIORITY port candidate — forms the override accountability subsystem.

name: log-session-activity
purpose: Logs session lifecycle events (session_start, session_end, file_write, file_edit, skill_invoke, commit) to .claude/session-activity.jsonl; supports --summary and --clear modes.
notes: module_system: cjs. JSONL target: .claude/session-activity.jsonl. Event allowlist validated at runtime: {session_start, session_end, file_write, file_edit, skill_invoke, commit}. Implements full secret redaction pipeline on all logged values (token patterns, bearer/basic auth, key=value). Uses security-helpers.js isSafeToWrite (not symlink-guard directly). Size-based rotation at 100KB. Does NOT use entry-based rotation or rotate-state.js (simpler than log-override.js). Exports nothing — pure entry-point script. HIGH-PRIORITY port candidate for session lifecycle tracking.

name: normalize-canon-ids
purpose: Normalizes CANON-*.jsonl audit finding files to sequential CANON-XXXX IDs; two-pass algorithm (build mapping, rewrite references); protects against duplicate canonical_id detection.
notes: module_system: esm (mixed — uses createRequire for safe-fs and parse-jsonl-line). JSONL target: docs/reviews/2026-Q1/canonical/CANON-*.jsonl — hardcoded default directory is SoNash-specific. Sorts by: severity (S0→S3), consensus, confidence, effort. Exports nothing; pure entry-point. not-portable because it is tightly coupled to SoNash's CANON audit finding schema and docs/reviews directory structure.

name: phase-complete-check
purpose: Automated phase completion gate: runs ESLint + tests, audits deliverables from a plan file, and (in interactive mode) runs 7 manual verification questions before allowing a phase to be marked complete.
sanitize_fields: npm run lint command (SoNash-specific lint script)
docs/archive path for archived deliverable lookup
Manual verification question set (question #7 references /session-end — SoNash skill name)
notes: module_system: esm. Exports extractDeliverablesFromPlan, verifyDeliverable, runAutomatedDeliverableAudit for testing. Full symlink traversal protection (realpathSync canonicalization, lstatSync check, archive containment validation). Interactive mode requires TTY. The concept is fully portable; the npm script names and question wording are the sanitize points. MAX_CHECKS=20 deliverable limit applies only in interactive mode (auto mode checks all).

name: promote-patterns
purpose: CLI wrapper that loads and invokes the compiled TypeScript promote-patterns pipeline from scripts/reviews/dist/; promotes recurring code patterns from reviews.jsonl to CODE_PATTERNS.md.
notes: module_system: cjs. Thin wrapper — the real logic lives in scripts/reviews/ TypeScript. Requires cd scripts/reviews && npx tsc before use. not-portable because it wraps a SoNash-specific review promotion pipeline and TypeScript sub-project. No test for this wrapper itself; tests exist for the underlying TS module.

name: ratchet-baselines
purpose: Reads current pattern violation counts from check-pattern-compliance.js, compares against stored baselines in .claude/state/known-debt-baseline.json, ratchets baselines down on improvement, and exits non-zero on regression.
notes: module_system: cjs. State file: .claude/state/known-debt-baseline.json. Depends on check-pattern-compliance.js (not-portable, SoNash-specific). not-portable because the baseline system is tightly coupled to SoNash-specific patterns. Idempotent: running twice with no changes produces same result. Note: ratchet-baselines.js has BOTH a tests/ test (tests/scripts/ratchet-baselines.test.ts) AND a scripts/__tests__ test (scripts/__tests__/ratchet-baselines.test.js).

name: reclassify-learning-routes
purpose: One-time migration script that reclassifies learning-routes.jsonl entries by enforcement layer (deterministic/semi-deterministic/probabilistic) and updates their status fields.
notes: module_system: cjs. JSONL target: .claude/state/learning-routes.jsonl (SoNash-specific learning system). Marked as one-time migration (dated 2026-04-07). not-portable because it operates on SoNash-specific learning-routes.jsonl schema and enforcement layer taxonomy. No test file found in tests/scripts/ or scripts/__tests__.

name: refine-scaffolds
purpose: Reads scaffolded entries from learning-routes.jsonl, runs confidence-classifier, promotes high-confidence routes to 'enforced' status and routes low-confidence ones to pending-refinements.jsonl.
notes: module_system: cjs. JSONL targets: .claude/state/learning-routes.jsonl, .claude/state/pending-refinements.jsonl. Depends on confidence-classifier.js which is NOT in JASON-OS (porting gap). not-portable due to SoNash learning system dependency. Test file exists: tests/scripts/refine-scaffolds.test.ts.

name: render-orphan-report
purpose: Reads .planning/orphan-detection/findings.jsonl and renders a grouped Markdown report to .planning/orphan-detection/REPORT.md.
sanitize_fields: CATEGORY_ORDER array (SoNash orphan categories: scripts, workflows, hooks, state-files, agents, skills, docs, planning, research)
notes: module_system: cjs. Uses sanitize-error.js (note: imports from './lib/sanitize-error.js' not '.cjs' — check SoNash lib for this variant). The orphan detection concept is portable; the category taxonomy and paths are the sanitize points. No test file found.

name: reset-audit-triggers
purpose: Resets AUDIT_TRACKER.md trigger counters after audits complete; supports multi-ai, comprehensive, multi-ai-single, and single reset types with category-level granularity.
notes: module_system: esm (uses fileURLToPath/import.meta). VALID_CATEGORIES list is SoNash audit taxonomy (code, security, performance, refactoring, documentation, process, engineering-productivity, enhancements, ai-optimization). docs/audits/AUDIT_TRACKER.md is SoNash-specific. not-portable. Test file: tests/scripts/reset-audit-triggers.test.ts.

name: resolve-hook-warnings
purpose: Checks whether hook warning conditions are still active; marks resolved warnings in the JSONL log so they are excluded from the regenerated cache view; runs during session-start after warning regeneration.
notes: module_system: cjs. JSONL target: .claude/state/hook-warnings-log.jsonl (same target as append-hook-warning.js). Reads: .claude/state/hook-warnings-ack.json, .claude/hook-warnings.json. Resolve checks are registered per warning type — each is a function returning true if condition resolved. pr-creep resolve check uses git (branch commit count). Rated portable because all three state file paths follow conventional .claude/ layout. Uses defensive guarded imports for all deps (fail-loud pattern). No test file found.

name: review-lifecycle
purpose: Orchestrates the full review lifecycle pipeline: SYNC (markdown→JSONL), VALIDATE, RECONCILE-COMMITS, RECONCILE (dedup+round-counts), RENDER (markdown view); owns all writes to reviews.jsonl during session-start.
notes: module_system: cjs. JSONL targets: .claude/state/reviews.jsonl, .claude/state/reviews-archive.jsonl (deprecated 2026-04-17 — merged into reviews.jsonl), .claude/state/review-metrics.jsonl. Depends on render-reviews-to-md.ts TypeScript script. not-portable due to SoNash-specific review system and TypeScript sub-pipeline. Test: scripts/__tests__/review-lifecycle.test.js.

name: route-enforcement-gaps
purpose: Reads CLAUDE.md, extracts rules annotated with [BEHAVIORAL: no automated enforcement], and routes each through the learning-router to scaffold enforcement.
notes: module_system: cjs. Part of SoNash's Data Effectiveness Audit Wave 4.2. Reads CLAUDE.md from project root — the concept is theoretically portable but depends on SoNash-specific learning-router and learning-routes.jsonl schema. not-portable. Test: scripts/__tests__/route-enforcement-gaps.test.js.

name: route-lifecycle-gaps
purpose: Reads lifecycle-scores.jsonl, finds systems with Action < 2, and routes each gap through the learning-router for enforcement scaffolding.
notes: module_system: cjs. JSONL source: .claude/state/lifecycle-scores.jsonl (SoNash-specific). Uses read-jsonl.js (JASON-OS has this). Part of Wave 5.2 Data Effectiveness Audit. not-portable. Test: scripts/__tests__/route-lifecycle-gaps.test.js.

name: run-consolidation
purpose: JSONL-based pattern consolidation from reviews.jsonl to CODE_PATTERNS.md; state lives in .claude/state/consolidation.json and .claude/state/reviews.jsonl.
notes: module_system: cjs. JSONL target: .claude/state/reviews.jsonl, .claude/state/consolidation.json. Output: consolidation-output/suggested-rules.md (gitignored working dir). v2 of the script (v1 archived at scripts/archive/run-consolidation.v1.js). not-portable — tightly coupled to SoNash review/pattern system. Test: tests/scripts/run-consolidation.test.ts.

name: run-github-health
purpose: Quick GitHub health check (3 API calls: 1 GraphQL + 2 REST) for session-begin; outputs GREEN/YELLOW/RED status; appends results to github-health-history.jsonl.
sanitize_fields: Repository owner/name (hardcoded in gh API calls — SoNash repo slug)
notes: module_system: cjs. Uses sanitize-error.cjs (canonical .cjs wrapper — cannot use ESM require). JSONL target: .claude/state/github-health-history.jsonl. Dedup guard prevents duplicate runs within 30 minutes. CACHE_LIMIT_BYTES = 10GB (GitHub storage limit check). sanitize-then-portable: the pattern (gh health check + JSONL history) is generic; only the repo slug needs replacement. Test: tests/scripts/run-github-health.test.ts.

name: search-capabilities
purpose: Unified skill/plugin discovery — searches .claude/skills, .agents/skills, and Claude Code plugin marketplaces for capabilities matching a keyword query.
sanitize_fields: LOCAL_SKILLS_DIRS paths (.claude/skills and .agents/skills — may differ in consuming project)
notes: module_system: cjs. Reads skills from both .claude/skills and .agents/skills directories. Depends on scripts/config/load-config.js (SoNash-specific). The marketplace search concept is portable; the config loader dep is the blocker. Test: tests/scripts/search-capabilities.test.ts.

name: security-check
purpose: Quick security pattern compliance checker for modified/staged files; scans for execSync injection, eval, innerHTML, require with user input, etc.; runs via npm run security:check.
notes: module_system: esm (fileURLToPath/import.meta). SECURITY_PATTERNS array defines: SEC-001 (execSync injection), SEC-002 (eval), SEC-003 (innerHTML), and others. Checks staged files by default (git diff --cached), --all mode, or --file mode. --blocking flag enables non-zero exit on violations. Full symlink traversal protection (lstatSync, realpathSync). Exclude patterns per rule — some exclude the script itself to avoid self-flagging. Rated portable: no SoNash-specific content in the pattern set. Test: tests/scripts/security-check.test.ts.
